perturbation.original_text: (1) "Configuration" means the preparation of a computer and related ------------- hardware and integration of components into a computer system; provided -------- that the term "Configuration" shall not include installation of a computer or related hardware at a customer site.
perturbation.changed_text: (1) "Configuration" means the preparation of a computer and related ------------- hardware and integration of components into a computer system, including installation of a computer or related hardware at a customer site.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Configuration' explicitly excludes installation at a customer site. This modification directly contradicts that exclusion, creating ambiguity as to what is covered under 'Configuration'.  This change will impact the interpretation of other sections where the definition of configuration is being used.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (4) "Product Business" means the acceptance and fulfillment of ---------------- customer orders for Products, including the manufacturing, channel assembly, co-location or centralized image loading and Configuration of Products; provided that the term "Product Business" shall not include on- -------- site Configuration of computer or related
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly excludes installation at a customer site from the definition of 'Configuration'. The changed text includes it. The 'Contradicted Text' then excludes 'on-site Configuration of computer' from the definition of 'Product Business'. By changing the definition of configuration to include 'on-site installation', it directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, because 'on-site configuration' will sometimes be included and sometimes be excluded. This aligns with the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (5) "Outsourcing and Professional Services" means (a) consulting, ------------------------------------- system migrations, project management, other services typically referred to as "high-end" services, and (b) outsourcing contracts having a term of more than one year which require pricing be done on a per seat basis. Agreements that consist primarily of lower-end services, including, but not limited to, break/fix, IMAC, warranty and low-end staff augmentation, other than agreements priced on a per-seat basis, are not Outsourcing and Professional Services agreements.
perturbation.changed_text: (5) "Outsourcing and Professional Services" means (a) consulting, ------------------------------------- system migrations, project management, other services typically referred to as "high-end" services, and (b) outsourcing contracts having a term of more than one year which require pricing be done on a per seat basis. All agreements, including those that consist primarily of lower-end services, including, but not limited to, break/fix, IMAC, warranty and low-end staff augmentation, are Outsourcing and Professional Services agreements.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Outsourcing and Professional Services' specifically excluded lower-end services unless priced per seat. This modification removes that exclusion, now including all agreements regardless of the type of service provided, directly contradicting the original intent. This creates a contradiction regarding the scope of 'Outsourcing and Professional Services.'
perturbation.contradicted_location: ARTICLE III AGREEMENT NOT TO COMPETE 3.1 (b)
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Seller agrees that during the Non-Competition Period Seller will, and will cause its Subsidiaries to use commercially reasonable efforts to cause future Service customers to whom Seller provides Outsourcing and Professional Services and existing Service customers of Seller to whom Seller is able to expand its Services, to procure Products and image load/configuration from CompuCom in accordance with the provisions of Article IV, provided that CompuCom is not competing with Seller to provide future or expanded Services to such customers.
perturbation.justification: The original text excludes lower-end services from the definition of 'Outsourcing and Professional Services' unless priced per seat, while the changed text includes all agreements regardless of service type. This contradicts the contradicted text, which references 'Outsourcing and Professional Services' in the context of seller's obligations to procure Products, implying that some services would *not* fall under this category. The modification makes the definition all-encompassing, thus changing the scope of the obligation and creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (7) "Services" means all IT services offered by Seller, including, -------- but not limited to, all outsourcing, professional services, break/fix, staff augmentation and consulting services; provided that the term -------- "Services" shall not include (i) on-site Configuration of Products by CompuCom or (ii) the sale (but not the performance) by CompuCom of extended warranty contracts at time of initial sale of Products to customers.
perturbation.changed_text: (7) "Services" means all IT services offered by Seller, including, -------- but not limited to, all outsourcing, professional services, break/fix, staff augmentation and consulting services; including (i) on-site Configuration of Products by CompuCom and (ii) the sale (but not the performance) by CompuCom of extended warranty contracts at time of initial sale of Products to customers.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Services' explicitly excludes (i) on-site Configuration of Products by CompuCom or (ii) the sale (but not the performance) by CompuCom of extended warranty contracts at time of initial sale of Products to customers. This modification directly contradicts that exclusion, creating ambiguity as to what is covered under 'Services'. This can create contradiction in Article III sections a and b, as it is talking about what service CompuCom can give.
perturbation.contradicted_location: ARTICLE III AGREEMENT NOT TO COMPETE 3.2 (a)
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 CompuCom's Agreement. (a) CompuCom agrees that during the Non- -------------------- Competition Period CompuCom will not, and will not permit any of its Subsidiaries to: (i) provide any Services to any account listed on Exhibit B hereto; ---------
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly excluded 'on-site Configuration of Products' and 'the sale (but not the performance) by CompuCom of extended warranty contracts' from the definition of 'Services'. The changed text includes these items within the definition of 'Services'. The contradicted text states that CompuCom will not provide any 'Services' to accounts listed on Exhibit B during the Non-Competition Period. Since the changed text includes items that were previously excluded in the definition of 'Services', it now contradicts the contradicted text, because CompuCom is now restricted from doing something that it was previously explicitly allowed to do.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 Seller's Agreement. (a) Seller agrees that during the Non- ------------------ Competition Period Seller will not, and will not permit any of its Subsidiaries to: (i) engage in the Product Business; (ii) encourage any Service Account to specify a party other than CompuCom to provide Products and image load/configurations to such Service Account; or (iii) solicit, entice or induce any employee of CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom to terminate his or her employment with CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom or hire any person who was or is at any time from the date of execution of the Asset Purchase Agreement to the end of the Non-Competition Period an employee of CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 Seller's Agreement. (a) Seller agrees that during the Non- ------------------ Competition Period Seller will make reasonable effort not to, and will not permit any of its Subsidiaries to: (i) engage in the Product Business; (ii) encourage any Service Account to specify a party other than CompuCom to provide Products and image load/configurations to such Service Account; or (iii) solicit, entice or induce any employee of CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom to terminate his or her employment with CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom or hire any person who was or is at any time from the date of execution of the Asset Purchase Agreement to the end of the Non-Competition Period an employee of CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'make reasonable effort not to' weakens Seller's commitment, introducing ambiguity. Originally, Seller 'will not' engage in prohibited activities. The new phrasing makes enforcement unclear. This introduces an internal contradiction where the 'Agreement' previously states no competition, it creates an internal conflict with this new definition.
perturbation.location: ARTICLE III AGREEMENT NOT TO COMPETE 3.1 (a)
perturbation.contradicted_location: ARTICLE III AGREEMENT NOT TO COMPETE 3.1 (a)
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 Seller's Agreement. (a) Seller agrees that during the Non- ------------------ Competition Period Seller will make reasonable effort not to, and will not permit any of its Subsidiaries to: (i) engage in the Product Business; (ii) encourage any Service Account to specify a party other than CompuCom to provide Products and image load/configurations to such Service Account; or (iii) solicit, entice or induce any employee of CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom to terminate his or her employment with CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom or hire any person who was or is at any time from the date of execution of the Asset Purchase Agreement to the end of the Non-Competition Period an employee of CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the 'Seller will not' engage in certain activities, establishing a firm obligation. The changed text introduces 'make reasonable effort not to,' which weakens the obligation and creates ambiguity. The contradicted text, which is identical to the Changed Text shows this new modified obligation, creates a direct conflict with the initial commitment to abstain entirely from the actions. Because the parties now only need to 'make reasonable effort not to' perform the actions, there is a direct contradiction of a firm commitment. Therefore, according to the definition of Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction, a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: CompuCom agrees to notify Seller of CompuCom's rejection of such orders for Product within a mutually agreed upon time that is reasonable under the time constraints required by the customer order or contract.
perturbation.changed_text: CompuCom may, at its discretion, notify Seller of CompuCom's rejection of such orders for Product whenever CompuCom deems it appropriate.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires CompuCom to notify Seller of rejections within a 'mutually agreed upon time.' The modified text gives CompuCom complete discretion over notification, making it unclear if and when Seller will be informed. This conflicts with the expectation of timely communication established in the original agreement. This creates ambiguity with regard to the time constraint defined in the previous sentences.
perturbation.location: ARTICLE IV Acceptance of Product Orders
perturbation.contradicted_location: ARTICLE IV Acceptance of Product Orders
perturbation.contradicted_text: CompuCom agrees that during the Non-Competition Period, it will accept orders for Products submitted to CompuCom by Seller pursuant to Section 3.1(b) under the following conditions: (i) CompuCom has the ability to offer such Product for sale; (ii) the purchase price required by the customer order for the Product is reasonably acceptable to CompuCom; (iii) such Product offered by CompuCom meets the customer's specifications, including, but not limited to, configuration specifications; and (iv) the requirements of the customer with respect to such Product, including but not limited to delivery, payment terms and return privileges are reasonably acceptable to CompuCom. CompuCom may, at its discretion, notify Seller of CompuCom's rejection of such orders for Product whenever CompuCom deems it appropriate.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that CompuCom must notify the seller within a 'mutually agreed upon time', but the changed text grants CompuCom discretion to notify 'whenever CompuCom deems it appropriate'. The Contradicted Text (which is the same as the Changed Text) reinforces this discretionary notification. This directly contradicts the original obligation of a mutually agreed upon timeframe, because that timeframe is now open to interpretation. Therefore, the change creates a strong in-text contradiction, since the seller doesn't have the requirement to follow the 'mutually agreed upon time'.

perturbation.original_text: 6.5 Specific Performance. The parties acknowledge that money damages -------------------- are not an adequate remedy for violations of this Agreement and that any party may, in its sole discretion, apply to a court of competent jurisdiction for specific performance or injunctive or such other relief as such court may deem just and proper in order to enforce this Agreement or prevent any violation hereof and, to the extent permitted by applicable law, each party waives any objection to the imposition of such relief.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.5 Specific Performance. The parties acknowledge that money damages -------------------- are the adequate remedy for violations of this Agreement and that any party may, in its sole discretion, not apply to a court of competent jurisdiction for specific performance or injunctive or such other relief as such court may deem just and proper in order to enforce this Agreement or prevent any violation hereof and, to the extent permitted by applicable law, each party does not waive any objection to the imposition of such relief.
perturbation.explanation: The original section specifies that monetary damages are *not* an adequate remedy and parties *may* seek specific performance. The altered text now states monetary damages *are* an adequate remedy and parties *may not* seek specific performance. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the enforceability of the agreement beyond monetary compensation. This contradicts the remedies cumulative clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.6 Remedies Cumulative. All rights, powers and remedies provided ------------------- for under this Agreement or otherwise available in respect hereof at law or in equity shall be cumulative and not alternative, and the exercise or beginning of the exercise of any thereof by any party shall not preclude the simultaneous or later exercise of any other such right, power or remedy by such party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 6.5 directly contradicts the original text by stating that money damages *are* an adequate remedy, and parties *may not* seek specific performance. This clashes with Section 6.6, which states that all remedies are cumulative and not alternative. Therefore, the modified text creates a strong in-text contradiction by limiting the available remedies and conflicting with the cumulative remedies provision.
